sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to salt used as an ingredient of food, both for direct sale to the consumer and for food manufacture. It applies also to salt used as a carrier of food additives and/or nutrients. Subject to the provisions of this standard more specific requirements for special needs may be applied. It does not apply to salt from origins other than those mentioned in Section 2, notably the salt which is a by-product of chemical industries.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Food grade salt is a crystalline product consisting predominantly of sodium chloride. It is obtained from the sea, from underground rock salt deposits or from natural brine.

sections.title: USE AS A CARRIER
sections.text: Food grade salt shall be used when salt is used as a carrier for food additives or nutrients for technological or public health reasons. Examples of such preparations are mixtures of salt with nitrate and/or nitrite (curing salt) and salt mixed with small amounts of fluoride, iodide or iodate, iron, vitamins, etc., and additives used to carry or stabilize such additions.

sections.title: PACKAGING, TRANSPORTATION AND STORAGE

page_spans.text: In any salt iodisation program, it is important to ensure that salt contains the recommended amount of iodine at the time of consumption. The retention of iodine in salt depends on the iodine compound used, the type of packaging, the exposure of the package to prevailing climatic conditions and the period of time between iodisation and consumption. sections.title: LABELLING OF NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS
sections.text: Information for non-retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

sections.title: If necessary in order to avoid the loss of iodine, iodised salt should be packed in air tight bags of either high density polyethylene (HDPE) or polypropylene (PP) (laminated or non-laminated) or LDPE-lined jute bags (Grade 1803 DW jute bags lined with 150 gauge polyethylene sheet). In many countries, this may require a major switch from conventional packaging materials made of straw or jute. The cost of adding extra iodine to compensate for its loss from cheaper packaging (i.e., straw or jute) must be weighed against the cost of switching to the above expensive packing material.

sections.title: DETERMINATION OF SODIUM CHLORIDE CONTENT
sections.text: This method allows the calculation of sodium chloride content, as provided for in Section 3.1, on the basis of the results of the determinations of sulphate (Method 9.4), calcium and magnesium (Method 9.5), potassium (Method 9.6) and loss on drying (Method 9.7). Convert sulphate to CaSO 4 and unused calcium to CaCl 2, unless sulphate in sample exceeds the amount necessary to combine with calcium, in which case convert calcium to CaSO 4 and unused sulphate first to MgSO 4 and any remaining sulphate to Na 2SO4. Convert unused magnesium to MgCl 2. Convert potassium to KCl. Convert unused halogens to NaCl. Report the NaCl content on a dry matter basis, multiplying the percentage NaCl by 100/100 -P, where P is the percentage loss on drying.

sections.text: This method represents a variables sampling procedure for mean quality:  blended bulk sample analysis. sections.text: The size and the number of the items forming the sample depend on the type of salt and the lot magnitude. sections.text: If the N units in the lot have been classified and can be numbered from 1 to N, the 1 -in-k systematic sampling of n items can be obtained as follows: a) Determine the k value as k = N/n.  (If k is not an integer, then round to the nearest integer). b) From the first k items in the lot take one at random and then take every kth item thereafter.
sections.retrieval_text:
APPENDIX > METHOD FOR THE SAMPLING OF FOOD GRADE SALT FOR THE DETERMINATION OF SODIUM CHLORIDE > PROCEDURE > PREPACKED SALT > Systematic sampling
number=6.1.2
level=3
If the N units in the lot have been classified and can be numbered from 1 to N, the 1 -in-k systematic sampling of n items can be obtained as follows: a) Determine the k value as k = N/n.  (If k is not an integer, then round to the nearest integer). b) From the first k items in the lot take one at random and then take every kth item thereafter.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR FOOD GRADE SALT
pages.summary: A página contém a estrutura completa dos capítulos 1 a 7 da norma CODEX STAN 150-1985 para food grade salt, com seções e subseções numeradas hierarquicamente até nível 3 claramente identificadas e textos associados. A seção 7 LABELLING continua na próxima página, evidenciado pelo atributo continues_on_next_page.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 150-1985
pages.summary: A página 2 do documento CODEX STAN 150-1985 apresenta os capítulos 7, 8 e 9 com suas respectivas subseções numeradas hierarquicamente, contendo principalmente títulos e trechos introdutórios. A seção 7 LABELLING continua da página anterior, e a seção 9 METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING segue na próxima página. A estrutura está organizada até nível 3, com associação clara de textos explicativos e marcação de continuidade adequada para uso em RAG.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 150-1985
pages.summary: Página 3 do documento CODEX STAN 150-1985 apresenta subseções do capítulo 9 METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING numeradas de 9.6 a 9.13, com textos explicativos associados. O conteúdo continua da página anterior (capítulo 9) sem indicação de continuação para a próxima página.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 150-1985
pages.summary: Página com o apêndice sobre o método de amostragem do sal alimentício para determinação de cloreto de sódio, contendo seções numeradas de 1 a 6 com textos explicativos e subseções detalhadas da seção 6. Texto estruturado em níveis hierárquicos claros, sem indicação de continuidade da página anterior, mas com possibilidade de continuação para a próxima página.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 150-1985
pages.summary: A página apresenta duas seções principais claramente demarcadas e numeradas: '7. ACCEPTANCE CRITERION' e '8. SAMPLING REPORT'. Ambas as seções incluem texto explicativo diretamente associado, com detalhamento em formato de itens e fórmulas matemáticas. Não há indicação clara de continuidade de conteúdo vindo da página anterior ou seguindo para a próxima, e o cabeçalho indica título do documento e número da página. Estrutura mapeada para uso em RAG com níveis 1 e conteúdo associado.
